Cubbard Route 9W — Derived Answers Workbench v0.15 Equipment Turning + DOT/SWPPP Math Studio

Enhanced answer-derivation and pre-application workbench integrating the v0.6 Cesium planning-board simulator, the Mapbox token from the attached Artemis script, Gemini dashboard concepts, Gemini pre-application brief, prior v0.7 answer cards, and a manual true-north/map-reconciliation control and an attached site-plan trace layer for the Cubbard overlay, plus startup orbit, speed-adjustable conceptual truck/equipment motion, equipment envelopes, turning/radius checks, NYSDOT access-screen logic, and SWPPP/rainfall math in KaTeX-style notation. It remains conceptual only: no legal survey, zoning determination, SWPPP, NYSDOT approval, septic/fire-flow approval, or purchase recommendation is claimed.

Scenario & Inputs

Mapbox 3D enabled. The workbench now embeds the public Mapbox token extracted from the attached Artemis script. The token is used only client-side for this local/demo map.

Cost Inputs

Confidence rule: model and listing data are not professional evidence. Every answer is tagged A/B/C/D or required/professional.

Mapbox 3D Site Map

Token source: public Mapbox pk token from the attached Artemis v0.9 script. The map uses the refined Cubbard GeoJSON controls from the planning-board simulator and overlays the active scenario's derived metrics.

Mapbox/WebGL did not initialize

The derived-answer tables and SVG site diagram still work. Check internet access, browser WebGL support, token URL restrictions, or ad/script blockers.

Mapbox pk token loaded

Manual Map Reconciliation

↑ True North / WGS84 Initial placement assumes the KML/GeoJSON coordinates are already north-up and correctly georeferenced. The satellite image is not changed; this tool rotates and nudges only the Cubbard overlay.

90.00° 0 ft 0 ft 1.000×
How to use: rotate the Cubbard model through the full 360° reconciliation range, shown as -180° to +180° around true north. Match the model road/reference strip, driveway throat, and Cubbard/Route 9W edges to the visible satellite imagery. The basemap stays fixed; only the model overlay rotates/nudges/scales. Do not treat this as survey correction; export the rotation/offset and give it to the surveyor/engineer as a reconciliation note.
Loading refined KML boundary, Route 9W reference, building footprint, equipment yard, stormwater placeholders, truck court, swept-path envelope, and KML pins.

Motion Studio

Startup demo: Auto-start is enabled. The site begins in a slow orbit with conceptual logistics animation. Use this as a planning-board / investor visualization layer only.

1.00× 8°/s
Inbound truck Outbound truck Loader Excavator Crane / lowboy Forklift
Operational stops: entry gate → check-in/staging → warehouse loading face → service yard/equipment loop → exit throat. Dwell times are visual placeholders and must be replaced with real truck-turning, gate-stacking, fire-access, and loading-dock design data.
Rotation correction: The overlay rotation is now a full-bearing control from -180° to +180°. That is equivalent to 360° total rotation authority. Positive values rotate the plan/model overlay clockwise; negative values rotate it counterclockwise. The satellite map is not rotated. Only the Cubbard overlay, attached plan trace, and plan image are transformed around the selected site pivot.
Regulatory screen source note: The NYSDOT and stormwater sections are a pre-application screen. NYSDOT commercial access guidance and EPA construction-stormwater thresholds were checked during this update; final local, state, and civil-engineering requirements still control.
New in v0.15: the default overlay orientation remains 90°. Motion Studio remains intact, and a new engineering screen adds rough heavy-civil equipment dimensions, turning/radius checks, Route 9W / NYSDOT access conditions, editable rainfall inputs, and SWPPP/runoff calculations rendered in KaTeX-style notation. This remains a visual planning aid only — not AutoTURN, not traffic engineering, and not a simulation of legally approved circulation.
v0.12.2 stability decision: this version restores the cleaner v0.11.1 Mapbox 3D + full-bearing reconciliation workflow as the primary experience. The later v0.12.1 over-expanded the model and changed too many defaults. This build keeps the previous layout, token pattern, full -180° to +180° rotation, plan image toggle, attached plan trace, and derived-answer logic without replacing the model with the heavier QA dashboard.
Attached plan update v0.11: The workbench now includes a plan-photo scenario and a separate attached-plan trace layer. The plan image is treated as a photographed reference exhibit, not a survey-grade CAD source. The visible central building label appears to read approximately 31,600 sf; this value is loaded as a model assumption until the original PDF/CAD or a clean scan confirms the exact number.

What was extracted from the attached plan photo

MODEL ASSUMPTION PROFESSIONAL REQUIRED
  • Large central proposed building / metal building footprint, visually labeled around 31,600 sf.
  • Route 9W shown at the bottom of the sheet with the main entry/exit throat toward the lower-right side.
  • One-way circulation arrows around the building and parking fields.
  • Parking rows on the front, upper, and right portions of the plan.
  • Stormwater pond / basin shown along the lower portion of the site, with bioretention/stormwater areas shown elsewhere.
  • 2-ft-style contour/grading information appears on the plan, but this photo is not clean enough for reliable slope digitizing.

Control rule: The attached plan trace is loaded as a separate overlay. Use the Reconcile Model tool to rotate/nudge/scale it against the satellite map. A final model should use the original signed PDF/CAD, survey control, north arrow, scale bar, and at least two known tie points.

  • Blue hull = refined KML control hull / study boundary, not a legal survey.
  • Initial orientation is only as reliable as the KML/GeoJSON control points. A mismatch with satellite roads means the overlay must be reconciled, not assumed correct.
  • The attached plan-photo trace is intentionally separated from the older refined KML concept layer so differences can be seen and reconciled instead of hidden.
  • 3D extrusions are conceptual measurement aids, not building-permit geometry.
  • Derived answers remain controlled by the editable scenario parameters at left.

Quick Site Diagram

Ask / Lookup

Type a question or click a preset. The workbench derives answers from the active parameters and evidence flags.

Scenario Compare

Compares the prior 20,900 sf placeholder, the refined KML footprint, and the high-disturbance dashboard screen. This resolves conflicting attached values by making them explicit scenarios instead of mixing them silently.

Geometry + Surface / Volume Derivations

3D Measurement Logic Placeholder

This area represents the derivation layer that can later mount the Cesium/Three.js viewer. The current calculations are deterministic from the parameter table.

Truck + Access Logic

Equipment Turning + NYSDOT / SWPPP Math Studio

Engineering status: these are screening calculations. Final approval needs survey/CAD control, NYSDOT commercial access review, vehicle-tracking software, civil drainage design, and a prepared SWPPP when thresholds are met.

Representative heavy-civil equipment envelopes

Equipment dimensions are rough planning envelopes, intentionally conservative. Replace with exact manufacturer cut sheets, trailer configuration, stinger/jeep/booster data, and actual delivery truck geometry before using this for permitting.

Turning / radius / ingress-egress screening

NYSDOT commercial access / Route 9W conditions

Route 9W is treated as a state-highway access risk. Any new/modified commercial access, change of use, work in ROW, driveway geometry, drainage connection, signing, traffic control, and possibly traffic-impact analysis should be handled through NYSDOT before relying on this layout.

SWPPP / rainfall / runoff screening

Rainfall inputs are editable placeholders. Use NOAA Atlas 14 / PFDS and the New York stormwater design methodology selected by the project civil engineer for final sizing.

Stormwater / SWPPP Screen

Engineered stormwater management is likely required if construction disturbance exceeds one acre. Final BMP type depends on hydrology, soils, groundwater, wetland limits, outlet conditions, and Town Engineer review.
